output_interface: Multiple communication interfaces (details not publicly specified)
compatible_drones: Platform-agnostic; designed for small UAS defense applications
price_source: Not publicly listed; described as 'about half that of competing gimbals.' Contact sales@uxvtechnologies.com for pricing.
notable_features: Ultra-light at 450 grams with multi-sensor payload
640x480 LWIR thermal sensor at up to 60 Hz
Gas-filled direct-drive rotation on yaw and pitch axes
Absolute encoders for precise motion control
Onboard NVIDIA Ampere GPU (1024 CUDA cores, 16GB RAM, 32 Tensor Cores)
Edge AI and advanced onboard analytics
Onboard video processor for mission-critical image processing
near_miss_reason: Exceeds 10x optical zoom (18x) and is well under 1000g (450g), but price is not publicly available. Described as 'about half competing gimbals' which could place it under $10,000 but cannot be confirmed.
research_notes: UXV Technologies is based in Copenhagen, Denmark. The GMB-450 is a defense-grade multi-sensor gimbal integrating RGB, LWIR thermal, and LRF in a 450g package. The 2-axis gimbal uses gas-filled direct-drive motors with continuous 360-degree rotation. It features an onboard NVIDIA Ampere GPU for edge AI processing. A datasheet PDF exists but detailed specs like dimensions, power consumption, and voltage are not available in public sources. Price is quote-based but positioned as roughly half the cost of competitors.
